Thanks, Mateo

Biggest line items this quarter are the B&H order (the new 24-70 and a Profoto mod) and FedEx insurance on the cookbook prints. Yes please export the shared card charges — that saves me a dig.

Thanks for the ping, Jesse

That ramen spot sounds amazing, I've been thinking about their spicy miso bowl for weeks. Should we say 12:30? And do you want to walk over together or just meet there?

body:
Hey Jesse, hope the morning shoot went well! I've wrapped up the second pass at the Estrela cookbook interior layout and would love your eyes before I send it to Ana. There are a few calls on the chapter-opener spreads I'm still unsure about and your photographer's read would really help.

I've shared the InDesign export to your email. The main areas I want feedback on are how the full-bleed plating shots sit against the recipe text and the new section divider treatment. Let me know if you have 30 minutes this week to go through it together or if async notes work better.

Mateo
snippet: Hey Jesse, hope the morning shoot went well! I've wrapped up the second pass at ...
subject: Estrela cookbook layout — your eyes?

body:
Hey Mateo, thanks for sharing! I took a quick look and the overall direction is really solid. The warmer paper tone on the dividers is a big improvement over v1 — it lets the food do the talking.

I do have thoughts on the chapter-opener spreads — specifically the full-bleed shots running right up against the recipe headers. I think the plating loses some breathing room when the text crowds it. Happy to jump on a call to walk through it. Does Thursday afternoon work, say around 2pm?

Jesse
snippet: Hey Mateo, thanks for sharing! I took a quick look and the overall direction is ...
subject: Re: Estrela cookbook layout — your eyes?

body:
Thursday at 2pm works perfectly, I'll send a calendar invite. And yes, you're right about the headers crowding the plating — I was wrestling with that myself. I tried a couple of approaches but wasn't happy with either.

I'll prep a few quick alternatives before the call so we can compare side by side. Also, if you get a chance beforehand, the dessert chapter opener on spread 12 could use a look too — I want to make sure the crop reads before I finalize anything.

Mateo
snippet: Thursday at 2pm works perfectly, I'll send a calendar invite. And yes, you're ri...
subject: Re: Estrela cookbook layout — your eyes?

body:
Great, accepted the invite. I'll block some time Wednesday evening to go through spread 12 and jot down notes so we can hit the ground running Thursday.

Looking forward to it — this version is shaping up really well. I think once we nail the chapter-opener crops it'll be in a great place to send to Ana.

Jesse
snippet: Great, accepted the invite. I'll block some time Wednesday evening to go through...
subject: Re: Estrela cookbook layout — your eyes?

body:
Hey, I finally looked into flights for the Barcelona trip. There are two decent options: a direct on July 14th leaving at 7am that gets us in around 8pm local, or a one-stop on the 15th that's about $180 cheaper per person but has a two-hour layover in Lisbon. I'm leaning toward the direct just to avoid the stress, but wanted to check with you first before booking anything.

Also, I found a great apartment in the Eixample neighborhood — 4 nights, rooftop terrace, good reviews. It's €220/night. The hotel I was looking at before is actually more expensive and doesn't feel as personal. Thoughts?
snippet: Hey, I finally looked into flights for the Barcelona trip. There are two decent ...
subject: Barcelona trip - flights and hotel

body:
The direct flight is a no-brainer for me — I'd rather not deal with a layover after a full work week. Go ahead and book it if the price still looks good.

The apartment sounds perfect honestly. Eixample is a great location and I'd much rather have our own space than a hotel. Can you send me the listing link so I can take a look? If you want to split the booking or put it on one card and I'll Venmo you, either works for me.

Also, did you figure out the car situation? I thought we were going to skip it and just use the metro.
snippet: The direct flight is a no-brainer for me — I'd rather not deal with a layover af...
subject: Re: Barcelona trip - flights and hotel

body:
Booked the flights — confirmation should be in your inbox shortly. I put them on my Amex so you can just Venmo me your half whenever, no rush.

Here's the apartment listing: [Airbnb — Casa Eixample with Terrace]. I'll go ahead and request it tonight. And yes, definitely skipping the car. The metro is super easy there and we can walk most of the touristy stuff anyway. I was thinking we rent bikes for a day if we want to do the park.

One thing — do you want to try to get Sagrada Família tickets in advance? I read they sell out weeks ahead.
snippet: Booked the flights — confirmation should be in your inbox shortly. I put them on...
subject: Re: Barcelona trip - flights and hotel

body:
Yes, absolutely get Sagrada Família tickets ahead of time. Last thing I want is to show up and not get in. Can you grab two for the morning of the 16th if they have availability? I'll cover those.

Bike day sounds fun, let's plan that for the 17th maybe. And I'm good with the apartment, go ahead and confirm it. I'll Venmo you for the flights this week.

Should we think about a dinner reservation anywhere? I know you had that place bookmarked from when your sister went.
snippet: Yes, absolutely get Sagrada Família tickets ahead of time. Last thing I want is ...
subject: Re: Barcelona trip - flights and hotel

body:
Got Sagrada Família tickets for the 16th at 9am — perfect way to start that day before the crowds hit. I'll forward the confirmation.

Yes! Bodega Sepúlveda — I just checked and they have a table for two at 8:30pm on the 17th. I went ahead and reserved it since it was the last slot. That works out nicely after the bike day.

I think we're pretty much set. Flight, apartment, Sagrada, dinner. I'll make a shared note with all the details so we have everything in one place.
snippet: Got Sagrada Família tickets for the 16th at 9am — perfect way to start that day ...
subject: Re: Barcelona trip - flights and hotel

body:
Hey love, forwarding this — the management company sent over the lease renewal ahead of our June 30th end date. They're offering another 12-month term on the Wythe Ave place. Rent goes up a little but honestly less than I expected for the neighborhood, and I really don't want to deal with moving and finding new natural light for your shoots.

They want a response this week. I'm on board to re-sign if you are — same split as now, $1,400 / $1,000. Want to look it over together tonight before I reply?

body:
Yes, let's re-sign. The south-facing windows alone are worth it — I'd never find that light again without paying way more, and half my client work gets shot right there in the apartment.

The small bump is fine, same split works for me. Let's read it over tonight after dinner just to check the parking-spot clause and the painting thing, then you can reply in the morning.

Love you, Jesse

body:

Main things from my side: I'll build the textured backdrop boards Monday night, and can you confirm the shot list with Ana so we know how many of the 25 deliverables are hero plates vs. detail? I'll bring the C-stands and the bounce. Want me to grab Jess Williams to style?

Mateo

body:
Mateo, Tuesday at 11am is perfect — that light through the front windows is exactly what these plates need. I confirmed the shot list with Ana: 25 deliverables, roughly 8 hero plates and the rest detail and texture shots, plus a couple of room ambiance frames for the cookbook.

Yes please grab Jess to style — her plating hands are so much steadier than mine. One thing to flag: I'll need the A7IV and the 90mm macro charged and the Profotos packed the night before. I can draft the call sheet today so everyone knows the 11am call time.

Jesse
snippet: Mateo, Tuesday at 11am is perfect — that light through the front windows is exac...
subject: Re: Estrela reshoot plan

body:

I'll prep the textured boards Monday night and load the car so we're not scrambling Tuesday morning. I'll bring coffee for the crew too — oat milk for you, obviously. Let me know if you want me to pick up any fresh props from the market on the way.

Mateo
